tickerText: [TENNIS] Wimbledon 2026 Day 4 Thursday, July 2: the second round concludes at the All England Club with defending champion Iga Świątek facing two-time major finalist Karolina Plíšková and 2022 winner Elena Rybakina meeting Caty McNally on Centre Court — after Day 3 saw Novak Djokovic dismantle Stefanos Tsitsipas 6-3, 6-4, 6-2 in 98 minutes and Jannik Sinner grind past Nuno Borges 7-6 (7-4), 7-6 (7-2), 6-4 to reach the third round • [SQUASH] HCL-SRFI PSA Challenger in Mumbai reaches semifinal Thursday at Juhu Vile Parle Gymkhana — top seed Velavan Senthilkumar faces Egypt's Yassin Shohdy and third seed Tanvi Khanna meets Japan's Akari Midorikawa after both Indians dominated their quarterfinals Wednesday, with Senthilkumar beating Abdullah Hafez 15-13, 11-4, 11-3 and Khanna outlasting Zoe Foo 11-6, 11-7, 8-11, 11-6 • [BADMINTON] YONEX Canada Open 2026 enters Round of 16 Thursday at Markham Pan Am Centre — Lee Zii Jia advanced when Kidambi Srikanth retired at 11-10 in game one, while home top seeds Victor Lai and Michelle Li headline the $250K BWF Super 300 draw as Canada's badminton stars chase ranking points on home soil through July 5 • [PICKLEBALL] Sansan PPA Asia 500 Tokyo Open Day 2 at Arena Tachikawa Tachihi — main-draw Round of 16 and quarterfinals across all five pro disciplines as 18-year-old Kiora Kunimoto, 15-year-old Tama Shimabukuro, and Beijing Open champion Sahra Dennehy headline the $50,000 stop with 805 players registered across pro and amateur brackets • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT United States Smash 2026 Round of 32 Thursday at the Ontario Convention Center — No. 2 seed Wang Manyu fell to Japanese wildcard Hitomi Sato 12-10, 11-4, 15-13 after Chen Yi's first-round exit, while Kanak Jha faces Tom Jarvis and Sun Yingsha meets Nina Mittelham as defending champion Wang Chuqin presses on at the $1.55M Grand Smash through July 5

newsItems.title: Świątek and Rybakina Headline Wimbledon Day 4 Centre Court
newsItems.excerpt: The Championships reach Day 4 on Thursday, July 2, as the second round in men's and women's singles concludes across the All England Club. Centre Court opens with a battle of former world No. 1s as defending champion Iga Świątek faces Karolina Plíšková, the 2021 Wimbledon runner-up who has won 20 of 29 matches since returning from a 16-month injury layoff. Two-time major champion Elena Rybakina then meets American Caty McNally, the only player to take a set off Świątek during last year's title run. Amanda Anisimova, last year's runner-up, faces 2020 Australian Open champion Sofia Kenin, while Alexander Zverev and Madison Keys also headline the outer courts. Day 3 delivered statement wins as seven-time champion Novak Djokovic dismantled Stefanos Tsitsipas 6-3, 6-4, 6-2 in 98 minutes and defending men's champion Jannik Sinner overcame a spirited Nuno Borges 7-6 (7-4), 7-6 (7-2), 6-4 to reach the third round, where he will face American Jenson Brooksby.

newsItems.title: Indian Duo Targets PSA Mumbai Finals on Semifinal Thursday
newsItems.excerpt: The HCL-SRFI PSA Challenger Squash Tournament reaches semifinal day Thursday at the Juhu Vile Parle Gymkhana Club in Mumbai. India's Velavan Senthilkumar, the men's top seed, faces Egyptian second seed Yassin Shohdy at 5:00 p.m. local time after dismantling fourth seed Abdullah Hafez 15-13, 11-4, 11-3 in Wednesday's quarterfinals. Women's third seed Tanvi Khanna meets Japan's sixth seed Akari Midorikawa at 4:45 p.m. after beating Malaysian eighth seed Zoe Foo 11-6, 11-7, 8-11, 11-6. Both Indians carry home-nation momentum into the last four of the $30,000 combined event, which has already produced upsets including Shameena Riaz's defeat of top seed Hayley Ward and Om Semwal's five-game win over Diego Gobbi before Semwal fell to Egypt's Ziad Ibrahim in the quarters. Finals are scheduled for Friday, July 3.

newsItems.title: Canada Open Reaches Round of 16 as Home Stars Take Centre Stage
newsItems.excerpt: The YONEX Canada Open 2026 enters Round of 16 play Thursday at the Markham Pan Am Centre in Ontario, with the $250,000 BWF Super 300 draw tightening after three days of main-draw action. Malaysia's Lee Zii Jia advanced when India's fourth seed Kidambi Srikanth retired trailing 11-10 in the opening game of their men's singles clash, giving Lee a measure of revenge after Srikanth beat him at the US Open last week. The spotlight now shifts to Canada's top seeds: Victor Lai, fresh off becoming the first Canadian to win a Super 1000 title at the Indonesia Open, and four-time Olympian Michelle Li, both chasing deep runs on home soil. Su Li Yang, Devika Sihag, and a contingent of Indian shuttlers remain in the draw as the tournament progresses toward quarterfinals on Friday and finals on Sunday, July 5.

newsItems.title: Wang Manyu Falls as Chinese Women Seeds Tumble at US Smash
newsItems.excerpt: Upsets continued to pile up in the women's singles at the WTT United States Smash 2026 as No. 2 seed Wang Manyu became the latest Chinese star to exit, losing 12-10, 11-4, 15-13 to Japanese wildcard Hitomi Sato in the second round at the Ontario Convention Center. Wang's defeat followed sixth seed Chen Yi's first-round loss to 43-year-old German veteran Han Ying, meaning two of China's top five women's seeds are already out of the $1.55 million Grand Smash. Top seed Sun Yingsha advanced with an 11-9, 11-3, 11-6 win over Yangzi Liu and next faces Nina Mittelham, while defending men's champion Wang Chuqin opened with an 11-6, 15-13, 11-3 victory over Zhou Qihao. American Kanak Jha carries home-court momentum into a Round of 32 clash with England's Tom Jarvis after his 11-6, 12-10, 4-11, 7-11, 12-10 upset of No. 14 Shunsuke Togami. The tournament runs through July 5.
